name: Verdant Health Commission Food Bank
description: Free groceries and fresh produce for families and individuals in need. No appointment needed. Serves all residents of South Snohomish County.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Verdant Health Commission is NOT a food bank. It is a Public Hospital District that funds community health services. The actual Lynnwood Food Bank is at 5320 176th St SW, phone (425) 745-1635. This entry should be replaced or removed.

name: Community Transit Access Program
description: Reduced-fare bus passes and paratransit services for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income residents.
eligibility: Age 65+, disability documentation, or income below 200% federal poverty level
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - All information confirmed via official sources

name: Edmonds School District Family Resource Center
description: Free parenting classes, youth programs, family counseling, and school supply assistance for families with children in Edmonds School District.
eligibility: Families with students in Edmonds School District
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Website URL corrected, other information appears accurate

name: Domestic Violence Services of Snohomish County
description: Emergency shelter, legal advocacy, and support services for survivors of domestic violence. 24/7 crisis hotline available.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and their children
_QA_STATUS: CORRECTED - Name changed from 'YWCA' to 'Domestic Violence Services of Snohomish County' (DVS). The phone number 425-252-2873 belongs to DVS, not YWCA. Website updated to dvs-snoco.org. Email may need verification.

name: Recreation Pavilion Senior Center
description: Daily activities, hot meals, fitness classes, and social programs for adults 55+. Drop-in welcome.
cost: Free or low-cost activities; lunch $5 suggested donation
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Name is misleading. This is the 'Recreation Pavilion', a multi-purpose community recreation facility serving ALL ages (pool, fitness room, preschool, etc.), not exclusively a senior center. Consider renaming to 'Recreation Pavilion' or clarifying it serves all ages with senior programs.

name: HealthPoint Community Health Center
description: Primary care, dental, mental health, and pharmacy services on a sliding fee scale. Accepts most insurance and uninsured patients.
eligibility: All patients welcome, sliding scale for uninsured
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - No HealthPoint location found at 21512 52nd Ave W, Mountlake Terrace. Nearest confirmed HealthPoint is in Bothell at 10414 Beardslee Blvd, phone (425) 486-0658. Address and phone need verification.

name: Mountlake Terrace Library
description: Free library services, computer access, WiFi, ESL conversation groups, homework help, and literacy programs.
_QA_STATUS: CORRECTED - Name changed to 'Mountlake Terrace Library' (part of Sno-Isle Libraries system, not Edmonds Library). Address corrected from 23600 to 23300 58th Ave W. Hours updated to match official website. Website updated to specific library page.

name: Northwest Immigration Rights Project
description: Free and low-cost immigration legal services including asylum applications, deportation defense, family petitions, and citizenship.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Information appears accurate. Note: NWIRP operates offices by appointment. Seattle office hours are Mon-Fri 9:30am-12pm and 1pm-4pm.

name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Snohomish County
description: After-school programs, homework help, sports, arts, and mentorship for youth ages 6–18.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Could not verify specific location at 6420 220th St SW, Mountlake Terrace. Boys & Girls Clubs of Snohomish County has 27+ locations but this specific address did not appear in search results. Website updated to bgcsc.org. Address needs verification via bgcsc.org/find-a-club/

name: Hopelink Emergency Services
description: Emergency food, energy assistance, utility bill help, and case management services.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Phone number (425) 889-7880 is for Hopelink's Kirkland location, not Bellevue. Bellevue location phone appears to be (425) 943-7555 for Food Pantry/Emergency Services or (425) 658-2592 for utility assistance. Address 14812 Main St, Bellevue is correct.

name: Volunteers of America Western Washington
description: Transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rental assistance for veterans and families experiencing homelessness.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Could not verify address 811 1st Ave, Suite 490, Seattle. The Colman Building (811 1st Ave) is a commercial office building with no apparent connection to VOA in search results. VOA main office appears to be in Everett at 2802 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201. Address and phone need verification.

name: Snohomish County Crisis Line
description: 24/7 mental health crisis support, suicide prevention, and referrals to local resources. Operated by Volunteers of America Care Crisis Response Services.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Description updated to clarify operated by VOA Care Crisis Response Services. Website updated to more specific crisis services page. Toll-free number also available: 1-800-584-3578.
